transcript: Hello. Uh, my name is Arnold. I'm CEO and co-founder of Sphere. Uh, and today I'd like to tell you a little story. Uh, it unfortunately is a tragedy. Uh, but it's a beautiful one. And uh, it's about everyone's favorite topic, stablecoins. Uh, and so, you know, first a little bit about uh Sphere. Uh, we are a B2B cross-border payments company. Uh, we help you move money from uh country A to country B faster and cheaper. And where we specialize is that it's really fast. We get pretty low level uh with the banking uh plumbing. And you can see on the right, uh by the time I finish this slide, inshallah, uh you uh will have been able to completely finish KYC uh and get started moving money. Um, but today we process billions of dollars and we got our start on Solana uh back in 2022, uh which I'll loop to back in a second here. Um, we mostly service emerging markets uh and various types of businesses. And there you go. The off-ramp's done. Uh, so, you know, uh, probably doesn't need to be said, but stablecoins are very big. Probably the more interesting point is that they've gotten big really quickly. Uh, and by 2030, they're estimated to be uh the world's default way of moving money. And, you know, uh when we first started Sphere back in '22, it was like widely considered a bad idea uh to make a stablecoin company. Uh, shortly thereafter, you know, USDC would depeg during the regional bank crisis. Uh, and frankly, due to luck more than anything, uh we've gotten an interesting perspective uh on everything from fintechs, early stage, uh mom-and-pop shops, all the way to some of the largest governments, uh financial institutions, and trade houses in the world. And so, uh we've seen some interesting things and I'd like to share uh one of those uh I suppose stories with you. What is everyone missing? Um, this story is about Antarctica. Uh, and Antarctica is a fish bone economy. Uh, this is how people buy and sell things. Uh, like most sovereign currencies, they have a fish bone printer. Uh, this is how they make more fish bones. And ultimately how they can control monetary policy. How much money is there, uh and how much money is coming in and uh ultimately leaving. Uh, the thing is, back in the 1990s, just like most emerging markets, Antarctica had a sovereign debt crisis. Uh, post World War II, a lot of countries came about uh and eventually it became a great place uh to invest money. Uh, but unfortunately, the pace of investment outgrew uh the rate of infrastructural development. And so unfortunately, uh like many of the coins that we know and love, uh Antarctica had this crisis and had to institute capital controls to protect their economy. One day, a new idea arrives. Uh, and it's the gift of stablecoins or stable dollars. Uh, which is amazing. Uh, I don't probably need to tell you about the revolutionary uh unlock that this represents. You can move money whenever you want. Uh, it is a way to get exposure to dollars, the world's reserve currency. It's interoperable. Uh, and there are a ton of things that I'm sure, you know, everyone throughout this conference has been speaking to you about. Uh, the thing is, there are a couple of types of economies. One might have normal local currency. Uh, one might have dollars already. Uh, the examples maybe of being Panama, Costa Rica, Singapore, Hong Kong. Uh, others might have strong institutions. Uh, these are all examples where stablecoins get injected and their economic uh after-effects. But the ultimate question, the, you know, elephant in the room, is if you have really easy access to dollars through stablecoins, why do you need the old fish bones at all? Uh, and it turns out that you kind of don't. So the way that uh Antarctica was previously paying their teachers, uh their police, their military, their government officials, uh those fish bones are now worthless. And so what do you do when no one wants to work, when the money that they're getting paid in uh is not worth as much? You know, what's happening underneath the memes of stablecoins, which is an incredibly innovative and great technology, uh is that fish bones are crashing. There's a mismatch in what people are earning and ultimately what they want to spend in. And for these countries like Antarctica, they lose start to lose control. Um, you know, the quiet part out loud, uh being mindful of time, uh is that stablecoins themselves are not the problem, uh but violent transitions are. And it's incumbent on us as an industry to think about what are the ways where we can deliver innovation responsibly. Um, you know, it's a separate question of whether or not there will one day be one currency ultimately for the world. Uh, but we've had an interesting peek uh into what happens to countries like Brazil, uh you know, throughout emerging markets when dollar access comes too quickly. Um, and so what we think about uh most of the uh I guess working days at Sphere is how do we deliver, help governments, regulators, uh financial institutions to get this responsible innovation, tap into the benefits of stablecoins uh without risking the collapse of their society over the coming decades. And if that's interesting to you, we'd love to talk. And hopefully this was uh an a different uh but hopefully interesting story about what stablecoins uh mean for the world. Thank you.

sources.title: Announcing Stellar X-Ray, Protocol 25
sources.snippet:
Stellar | Announcing Stellar X-Ray, Protocol 25
[](https://stellar.org/)
[Dev Docs](https://developers.stellar.org)
Menu
Developers
# Announcing Stellar X-Ray, Protocol 25
![]()
Author
Bri Wylde
Publishing date
It’s about that time again —a new protocol upgrade is on the horizon for the Stellar network: X-Ray! This upgrade begins laying the groundwork for developers to build configurable, compliance-forward privacy applications using zero-knowledge (ZK) cryptography without compromising the transparency that defines Stellar.
At Meridian 2025, Stellar Development Foundation’s (SDF) Chief Product Officer Tomer Weller outlined the long-term privacy strategy for Stellar (see the presentation[here](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=j36W1LUGGrs)). A core pillar of that strategy is a commitment to invest in critical privacy infrastructure and build the foundational cryptographic capabilities developers need before higher-level privacy solutions can exist.
X-Ray —named after the everyday tool used to show only what needs to be seen and nothing more —represents the first major milestone in that plan. It proposes native support for two important primitives:
* **BN254**, a widely used pairing-friendly elliptic curve, and
* **Poseidon**, a family of hash functions designed specifically for ZK proof systems
## Protocol timeline
* Testnet vote: January 7, 2026
* Mainnet vote: January 22, 2026
Follow progress and preparation instructions through the continuously updated[Protocol X-Ray Upgrade Guide](https://stellar.org/blog/developers/stellar-x-ray-protocol-25-upgrade-guide).
What X-Ray introduces and why it matters
## BN254: connecting Stellar to the broader ZK ecosystem
[CAP-0074: Host functions for BN254](https://github.com/stellar/stellar-protocol/blob/master/core/cap-0074.md)proposes adding native host functions that enable elliptic curve operations on BN254, the elliptic curve standard used across many well-known ZK applications, such as privacy pools, Lighter, Starknet, and ZK Email. While Stellar already supports BLS12-381, a newer pairing-friendly curve introduced in[CAP-0059: Host functions for BLS12-381](https://github.com/stellar/stellar-protocol/blob/master/core/cap-0059.md), BN254 remains the most widely used curve in today’s ZK ecosystem.
Without native Soroban support for BN-254, developers building ZK applications on Stellar have to either rewrite their applications for a different curve or maintain workarounds that add complexity and cost. CAP-0074 removes that friction by introducing[three host functions](https://github.com/stellar/stellar-protocol/blob/master/core/cap-0074.md#new-host-functions-introduced):
* `bn254\_g1\_add`
* `bn254\_g1\_mul`
* `bn254\_multi\_pairing\_check`
These functions enable efficient on-chain verification of zk-SNARK proofs, provide feature parity with Ethereum’s[EIP-196](https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-196)and[EIP-197](https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-197)precompiles, and make Stellar interoperable with existing ZK tooling and libraries. That means builders currently in EVM environments can easily migrate or extend projects to Stellar.
Supporting both BN254 and BLS12-381 helps enable configurable privacy on Stellar by giving developers the flexibility to choose the curve that best suits their goals.
What X-Ray introduces and why it matters
## Poseidon and Poseidon2: hash functions designed for ZK systems
While BN254 provides the pairing operations for proof verification, you also need an efficient cryptographic hash, which is where Poseidon comes in.
[CAP-0075: Cryptographic Primitives for Poseidon/Poseidon2 Hash Functions](https://github.com/stellar/stellar-protocol/blob/master/core/cap-0075.md)defines host functions that expose the core permutation primitives behind Poseidon and Poseidon2.
These primitives let developers design hash functions that are highly efficient in ZK circuits, addressing a key performance bottleneck in ZK systems. Traditional hashes like SHA-256 are computationally e

sources.title: Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform  | Drift Updates
sources.snippet:
Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform  | Drift Updates
[![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f0866da8eed4c/6867a1f64ca2c75f0d14f371_16c97ce9017b907297827c5113a068f9_Frame%201597880158.avif)](https://www.drift.trade/)
[
Launch App
](#)
## Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform
December 4, 2025
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f08e5798eed50/693023e13e4b72ada17e0fe2_2025-12-03%2017.19.13.jpg)
[
Contents
](#)
[
Contents
](#)
[
](https://twitter.com/driftprotocol)[
](https://discord.com/invite/fMcZBH8ErM)
[
Trade Now
](https://app.drift.trade/)
![]()
![]()
Drift v3 is the protocol’s biggest performance upgrade yet. Rebuilt around trader execution, v3 delivers**10x faster fills, 10x improved liquidity, and a cleaner, more intuitive interface**than v2.
This upgrade was built for one thing: outperformance. Instant execution. Deeper liquidity. A suite of tools that helps you trade faster and with more confidence.
Drift v2 was the first to bring cross-margin and multi-asset collateral to DeFi on-chain trading. We built on Solana to deliver NASDAQ-level performance on-chain and with major Solana upgrades like Alpenglow, Firedancer and Jito’s Block Assembly Market (BAM), Drift is built to outperform as the network scales.
Since launch, Drift has surpassed 1.5B in TVL, $133B+ in total trading volume and $650M in open interest. Drift v3 is built to take those numbers and your trading even further.
**Drift v3 will be available on Dec 4, 2025.**New users will need an invite code to access Drift v3.
**DM**[**@DriftProtocol**](https://x.com/DriftProtocol)**to request access.**
## Why v3?
Drift v3 represents the protocol’s next stage of maturity: the point where years of infrastructure, liquidity, and execution improvements converge into a faster, clearer, more reliable trading experience.
**Over the past year, Drift has evolved in three critical areas:**
1. Execution speed
2. Liquidity depth
3. Trader experience## How Drift v3 outperforms
*The road to v3 was focused on improving liquidity, trading execution and speed. Here’s what that looks like in practice:*
### **Trading Execution and Speed**
* **10x faster order fills:**On Drift v3, 85% of market orders are filled within the same slot. A slot is Solana’s smallest time unit (about 400 milliseconds). On Drift, that means that trades are executed in under half a second. Instant execution.
* **15x faster take profit/stop loss triggers:**For BTC, it dropped from 15 slots (\~6 seconds) to 1 slot (\~0.4 seconds). This means your risk controls and exits now trigger immediately, reducing slippage and missed opportunities.
* **Gasless trading by default:**Unlike other DEXs, where you pay gas on every trade, Drift abstracts gas fees so that’s one less thing to worry about eating into profits
* **400ms oracle updates**: Prices update every 400 millisecond (roughly 2.5 times per second). This keeps quotes accurate and reduces the chance of stale pricing.
* **Scales with Solana:**As Solana increases block size and throughput, Drift automatically gets faster too, handling more trades per second.### **Liquidity**
* **10x reduction in slippage on market orders:**In Drift v2, large market orders could move the price by 20 bps. In v3, that’s been cut to just 2 bps. Traders now get far better pricing, especially on size.
* **5x tighter TP/SL execution:**Take-profit and stop-loss orders are far more precise. On major markets like SOL and BTC, fills are 4x tighter, while non-majors see up to 5x improvement. This means exits trigger closer to the price you set, reducing losses and protecting gains.
* **Introduction of Drift Liquidity Provider (DLP)**: Provides liquidity for both Perps and Spot markets. Community members can deposit funds into DLP to support market-making and earn a share of the pool's profits. Currently in the testing stage. Public launch in Q1 2026.
* **Expanded Market Maker Program:**With v3’s expanded market maker initiatives, long-tail altcoins now hav

tags.impact_reason: Sphere, a B2B cross-border payments company that started on Solana, discusses stablecoins and their potential impact, presenting an interesting perspective, but not a groundbreaking update.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose the fish skeleton as the Semantic Anchor because the speaker explicitly uses the 'fish bone economy' as a powerful metaphor for a fragile sovereign currency. This visual directly represents the core theme of systemic collapse. I integrated the text 'FAILURE' as a structural crack in the skeleton's spine. This makes the abstract threat of 'state failure', mentioned in the context, a tangible and diegetic part of the visual's architecture.
visual_idea.description: In a cavernous, dark museum-like space, the massive skeleton of a fish is displayed. The skeleton is made not of bone, but of weathered, crumbling concrete, signifying fragile sovereign infrastructure. A deep, structural crack runs along the spine, subtly forming the word 'FAILURE'. Woven through the vertebrae and around the ribs are pristine, glowing fiber-optic cables, reinforcing the decaying structure and preventing its total collapse. The background is a deep, minimalist void, with faint architectural blueprint lines barely visible in the shadows, suggesting immense complexity.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A monumental, skeletal fish structure, crafted from weathered, crumbling concrete, stands majestically within a vast, dark, cavernous museum exhibit. The concrete shows deep textures, visible aggregate, and signs of extreme age and decay, embodying fragile sovereign infrastructure. A pronounced, structural crack slices along the spine of the concrete fish, subtly yet distinctly forming the word 'FAILURE', deeply etched and brittle. Interlaced intricately through the decaying vertebrae and around the ribs are pristine, intensely glowing fiber-optic cables, emanating a vibrant, cool light that reinforces the crumbling structure, preventing its imminent collapse. The cavernous background is a deep, minimalist void, with ethereal, faint architectural blueprint lines barely perceptible in the profound shadows, hinting at an underlying immense complexity. Illuminated by dramatic cinematic side-lighting, casting long, stark shadows and emphasizing extreme contrast. A focused spotlight diagonally highlights key elements, with high falloff, ensuring everything beyond the primary illumination fades into deep, mysterious darkness. Vertical composition (3:4 aspect ratio), accentuating epic height and monumental scale. The colossal fish skeleton dominates the frame, appearing towering and grand, with ample negative space above to amplify its immense presence within the expansive, dark museum environment. 8K, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealism, realistic materials, cinematic color grading, subtle film grain, dramatic shadows.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose the fish skeleton as the Semantic Anchor because the speaker explicitly uses the 'fish bone economy' as a powerful metaphor for a fragile sovereign currency. This visual directly represents the core theme of systemic collapse. I integrated the text 'FAILURE' as a structural crack in the skeleton's spine. This makes the abstract threat of 'state failure', mentioned in the context, a tangible and diegetic part of the visual's architecture.
visual_idea.description: A macro, 1:1 shot focusing on a single concrete vertebra of the fish skeleton. The frame is filled with the hyper-detailed, pitted texture of the dark, crumbling material. A single, brilliantly glowing fiber-optic cable snakes through a hole in the center of the vertebra, its internal light starkly contrasting with the surrounding decay. The interplay between the rough, aged concrete and the smooth, impossibly bright light is the entire focus.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A macro, 1:1 shot focusing on a single concrete vertebra of a fish skeleton, presented with the pristine isolation of a museum exhibit. The square frame is completely filled with the hyper-detailed, pitted texture of the dark, crumbling, rough, aged concrete material, showcasing every crevice and imperfection. A single, brilliantly glowing fiber-optic cable snakes elegantly through a central hole in the vertebra, its smooth, impossibly bright internal light starkly contrasting with the surrounding decay and dark shadows. The entire focus is on the dramatic interplay between the rough, aged concrete and the luminous, smooth light of the fiber optics. Cinematic side-lighting creates deep, high-contrast shadows and strong highlights, emphasizing the textures and the glowing element. The composition is symmetrical, with the vertebra centrally focused and tightly framed. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, ultra-detailed.

editorial.headline: Sphere Pivots to Sovereign Infrastructure: "Violent Transitions" Threaten Emerging Markets
editorial.standfirst: Sphere CEO Arnold Lee used his Breakpoint keynote to warn that rapid stablecoin adoption risks collapsing emerging market economies. The firm is now positioning itself as the critical middleware for governments to manage the shift from local fiat to digital dollars without triggering state failure.
key_takeaways: Sphere now processes billions of dollars annually in cross-border volume.
The firm is pivoting from pure B2B payments to "responsible innovation" advisory and infrastructure for governments.
Lee warned that unchecked dollarization (the "Antarctica" scenario) renders local currencies worthless, destroying monetary sovereignty.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
While most Breakpoint keynotes focused on TPS and validator clients, **Sphere** CEO Arnold Lee delivered a sobering macroeconomic warning: the rapid adoption of stablecoins is an existential threat to emerging market sovereignty. Using the metaphor of "Antarctica"—a fictional economy running on "fish bones"—Lee outlined the mechanism of currency substitution where easy access to digital dollars renders local fiat worthless. When a population rejects the sovereign currency in favor of USDC or USDT, the state loses its ability to fund essential services like education and defense, leading to what Lee termed a "violent transition."

This marks a significant strategic pivot for Sphere. Originally launched at a Solana hackathon in 2022 and now processing **billions of dollars** annually, the company is moving upstream from simple B2B cross-border rails to institutional and governmental infrastructure. The implicit pitch is no longer just about moving money cheaper; it is about offering a control layer for central banks and regulators. By positioning itself as the partner for "responsible innovation," Sphere aims to be the buffer that allows emerging markets to access dollar liquidity without suffering the total collapse of their domestic monetary policy.

The "Alpha" here is not a new widget, but a new market category: **Sovereign Stablecoin Infrastructure**. Lee’s speech suggests that the next wave of adoption won’t just be consumer wallets, but state-level integrations designed to prevent the "fish bone" scenario. For investors, Sphere is betting that as stablecoins become the default global settlement layer by 2030, the most valuable protocols will be those that help nation-states survive the transition, rather than those that simply bypass them.
